meta.title: Tax Credit Legislation Watchlist
meta.methodology: Curated federal tax-credit policy entries. Legislative status source_url fields are limited to official federal sources; market impact text is directional and should be re-reviewed quarterly.

entries.title: Permanent 12% increase in 9% LIHTC state ceiling
entries.pricing_impact: Expands competitive 9% credit authority beginning in 2026. More credit supply can modestly pressure cents-per-credit pricing lower unless investor demand rises with allocation volume.
entries.source_note: Public Law 119-21, section 70422(a), permanent state housing credit ceiling increase.

entries.title: 25% bond-financing test for 4% LIHTC buildings
entries.pricing_impact: Lowers the bond-financing threshold for qualifying buildings where post-2025 bond issues meet the statutory requirements. The change can widen 4% deal eligibility but may also add bond-credit supply that investors price selectively by market and sponsor risk.
entries.source_note: Public Law 119-21, section 70422(b), tax-exempt bond financing requirement.

entries.title: Permanent extension of the New Markets Tax Credit
entries.pricing_impact: Removes recurring extender risk for NMTC allocation planning after 2025. Pricing still depends on allocation competition, leverage structure, and investor appetite rather than statutory permanence alone.
entries.source_note: Public Law 119-21, section 70423, permanent extension of New Markets Tax Credit.

entries.title: Termination of 25C and 25D household clean-energy credits
entries.pricing_impact: Ends two consumer-side home energy credits after 2025, which can reduce after-tax support for owner-occupied retrofit and clean-energy upgrades. Treat homebuyer affordability or rehab assumptions after 2025 as requiring other subsidy sources.
entries.source_note: Public Law 119-21, sections 70505 and 70506, termination of energy efficient home improvement and residential clean energy credits.

entries.title: 45Y and 48E wind/solar credit termination deadlines
entries.pricing_impact: Wind and solar projects face tighter timing tests: construction generally must begin before the statutory deadline or be placed in service by the termination date. Transfer pricing should be discounted when execution risk could push a credit outside the protected window.
entries.source_note: IRS Notice 2025-42 summarizes Public Law 119-21 sections 70512 and 70513 and beginning-of-construction guidance.

entries.title: CRA regulations rescission notice of proposed rulemaking
entries.pricing_impact: The NPR creates uncertainty around future CRA examination treatment. Bank demand for LIHTC, NMTC, and HTC investments should be described as policy-sensitive until the agencies finalize a rule.
entries.source_note: Federal Register proposed rule, Community Reinvestment Act Regulations.

entries.title: Section 6418 transferability for eligible clean-energy credits
entries.pricing_impact: Allows eligible taxpayers to sell all or part of qualifying clean-energy credits for cash. Pricing is negotiated between buyer and seller and generally reflects credit type, tax year, seller credit quality, indemnity, insurance, and recapture risk.
entries.source_note: IRS elective pay and transferability guidance.

entries.title: Neighborhood Homes Investment Act
entries.pricing_impact: Would create a neighborhood homes tax credit for qualifying owner-occupied development and rehab if enacted. Do not size homebuyer subsidy from this proposal until statutory terms and allocation mechanics are enacted.
entries.source_note: 119th Congress House and Senate Neighborhood Homes Investment Act bill pages.
